---
title: "ACDC Analysis - IMPROVED FOR PUBLICATION"
author: "Namita Joshi"
date: "`r Sys.Date()`"
format:
  html:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 4
    toc-expand: 2   
    number-sections: true
    code-fold: FALSE
    code-folding: hide
    include-in-header: hypothesis.html
editor: visual
---

::: {style="display: flex; align-items: center; gap: 20px;"}
<img src="images/Era_Logo_FINAL_Colour.png" width="100" style="margin-top: 10px;"/>
:::

```{r setup, include=F}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  collapse = TRUE,
  comment = "#>",
  echo = TRUE,  
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

```{r packages & functions, include=F, echo=F}

if (!requireNamespace("pacman", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("pacman")
}
pacman::p_load(
  sf, knitr, rnaturalearth, rnaturalearthdata, 
  ggplot2, viridis, shiny, dplyr, treemap, treemapify, plotly, data.table,
  s3fs, arrow, devtools, gh, htmlwidgets,remotes,gridExtra, DT, tidyverse, waffle,
  ggridges, jsonlite, patchwork,cowplot,ordinal, stringr, metafor, lme4, lmerTest, miceadds,data.table, RColorBrewer, ggrepel, colorspace, kableExtra, ggnewscale, gridExtra, grid, performance, broom.mixed
)

# Set a directory for downloaded data
dl_dir <- "downloaded_data"

# Create the directory if it doesn't already exist
if(!dir.exists(dl_dir)){
  dir.create(dl_dir, showWarnings = FALSE)
}

# Should the era vocab be updated?
update_vocab<-F
```

```{r pre-load era compiled}

# Define local path
local_parquet <- "downloaded_data/era_compiled-v1.0-mh_2025-07-24.1-sc_2025_01_30.1-ie_2025_08_11.1-2025-09-09.1.parquet"

# Read it in
acdc_dat <- read_parquet(local_parquet)


```

```{r subset era data}
lac_countries<-c("Ecuador","Brazil","Peru","Guatemala","Colombia","Venuezela","Costa Rica",
                 "Honduras","Nicaragua","Haiti","Mexico","El Salvador")
products<-c("Maize","Common Bean","Coffee", "Arabica")

acdc_dat<-acdc_dat[Country %in% lac_countries]
acdc_dat<-acdc_dat[Product %in% products]
```

# Data exploration

```{r plot sites, echo=T, message=F}

# === 1) Load and filter ACDC data to target crops ===
target_crops <- c("Maize", "Common Bean", "Coffee")
outcome<- "Crop Yield"

acdc_dat <- acdc_dat %>%
  filter(Product %in% target_crops) %>%
  filter(Out.SubInd %in% outcome) %>%
  mutate(
    Latitude  = as.numeric(Latitude),
    Longitude = as.numeric(Longitude)
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(Latitude) & !is.na(Longitude))

sites_sf <- st_as_sf(acdc_dat, coords = c("Longitude", "Latitude"), crs = 4326, remove = FALSE)

# === 2) Load and clean the Dry Corridor shapefile ===
dry_corridor <- st_read("ERA-Agroecology_files/CorredorSeco.shp", quiet = TRUE) %>%
  st_make_valid() %>%
  st_transform(4326)

cat("✅ Loaded dry corridor with", nrow(dry_corridor), "features\n\n")

# Union all polygons into one outline
dry_corridor_union <- dry_corridor %>%
  st_union() %>%
  st_sf(geometry = .) %>%
  st_make_valid()

# === 3) Identify points in the Dry Corridor ===
inside_idx <- st_within(sites_sf, dry_corridor_union, sparse = FALSE)[, 1]

# Count distinct papers inside the corridor
corridor_papers <- sites_sf[inside_idx, ] %>%
  pull(Code) %>%
  unique() %>%
  length()

message("Dry Corridor contains ", corridor_papers, " distinct papers")

# === 4) Product-level counts (for table) and Country-level counts (for map) ===

# A) Crop counts (for the table - uses Product column)
country_counts <- acdc_dat %>% # Kept original name for Step 7 compatibility
  group_by(Product) %>%
  summarise(N_Papers = n_distinct(Code), .groups = "drop") %>%
  arrange(desc(N_Papers))

# B) Country counts (FOR THE MAP - requires harmonization and counting by Country)
# Assuming 'Country' is the column with the country name in acdc_dat
country_counts_for_map <- acdc_dat %>%
  mutate(
    # Harmonize country names in the data to match the map object names
    Country = case_when(
      Country == "United States of America" ~ "USA",
      Country == "Dominican Republic" ~ "Dominican Rep.",
      TRUE ~ Country
    )
  ) %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(N_Papers = n_distinct(Code), .groups = "drop")


# === 5) Load world basemap and join counts ===
world <- ne_countries(scale = "medium", returnclass = "sf") %>%
  filter(continent %in% c("North America", "South America")) %>%
  mutate(
    # Apply the same harmonization logic to the map data 'admin' column
    admin = case_when(
      admin == "United States of America" ~ "USA",
      admin == "Dominican Republic" ~ "Dominican Rep.",
      TRUE ~ admin
    )
  ) # Removed the 'rename(Product = admin)' that caused the join error

map_data <- world %>%
  select(admin, geometry) %>%
  # Corrected join: Join the map's 'admin' (country name) column to the count data's 'Country' column
  left_join(country_counts_for_map, by = c("admin" = "Country"))

# === 6) Build the map ===
map <- ggplot() +
  geom_sf(data = map_data, aes(fill = N_Papers), color = "white") +
  geom_sf(data = dry_corridor_union, fill = NA, color = "red", linewidth = 1) + # Use linewidth instead of size
  geom_point(data = sites_sf,
             aes(x = Longitude, y = Latitude),
             shape = 21, color = "black", fill = "white", size = 2, alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_fill_viridis_c(option = "mako", direction = -1, na.value = "gray95", name = "Papers per Country") +
  labs(fill = "Experiments in ERA") +
  coord_sf(xlim = c(-120, -30), ylim = c(-60, 35), expand = FALSE) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(legend.position = "bottom")

# === 7) Add Product table ===
table_grob <- tableGrob(country_counts)

# === 8) Arrange map and table side by side ===
final_plot<- grid.arrange(map, table_grob, ncol = 2, widths = c(2, 1))

# Create output directory
output_dir <- file.path("ERA-ACDC_analysis_files", "output", "publication_package")
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

ggsave(
  file.path(output_dir, "map.png"),
  plot = final_plot,
  width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)


# === plot for practices  ===

ag_counts <- acdc_dat%>%
  separate_rows(PrName, sep = "-") %>% 
  group_by(PrName) %>%
  summarise(Count = n_distinct(Code), .groups = "drop") %>%
  mutate(label = paste0(PrName, " (", Count, ")"))

# Define parts per square (e.g. 1 square = 10 studies)
parts_per_square <- 2

# Prepare waffle input
waffle_data <- ag_counts %>%
  mutate(
    squares = round(Count / parts_per_square)
  ) %>%
  filter(squares > 0)  # Drop any 0-square items

# Named vector for waffle
waffle_vec <- setNames(waffle_data$squares, waffle_data$label)

# Dynamically expand the "Blues" palette
blue_palette <- colorRampPalette(RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Blues"))(nrow(waffle_data))


waffle_plot_practices <- waffle::waffle(
  parts = waffle_vec,
  rows = 5,
  size = 0.5,
  colors = blue_palette,
  legend_pos = "bottom"
) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "bottom",
    legend.text = element_text(size = 10),
    legend.title = element_blank()
  ) +
  guides(fill = guide_legend(nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE))


waffle_plot_practices


```

![Systematic map of ERA in LAC. (a) Geographic locations of studies, (b) Distribution of products included by number of studies, (c) Distribution of technology groups for crop and livestock by number of studies with each square equal to \~5 papers, (d) Number of studies including outcome indicators related to farm productivity, resilience, and climate change mitigation.](ERA-ACDC_analysis_files/figure-html/combined_figure1.png){width="709" height="435"}

# meta-analysis

```{r meta-analysis, echo=T, message=F}
source("ERA-ACDC_analysis_files/lnrr_naka.R")
source("ERA-ACDC_analysis_files/v_lnrr_naka.R")
source("ERA-ACDC_analysis_files/aggregate_names.R")
source("ERA-ACDC_analysis_files/OutCalc.R")

## -------------------------------
## 1) Codes & output dir
## -------------------------------
load("ERA-ACDC_analysis_files/OutcomeCodes.rda")

start_wd <- file.path("ERA-ACDC_analysis_files", "output")

# Aggregation choice
# aggregation <- "no_aggregation"
# aggregation <- "combo"
aggregation <- "combo_solo"

savename   <- paste0("results_", aggregation)
output_dir <- file.path(start_wd, savename)
if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)

# Create publication package directory RIGHT HERE
pub_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "publication_package")
if (!dir.exists(pub_dir)) dir.create(pub_dir, recursive = TRUE)

setwd(output_dir)
cat("Saving outputs to:", output_dir, "\n")

## -------------------------------
## 2) Apply aggregation
## -------------------------------
# expects 'acdc_dat' already in your environment
if (aggregation == "no_aggregation") {
  acdc_dat_p <- as.data.table(acdc_dat)
} else {
  aggregated <- aggregate_names(
    Data           = acdc_dat,
    CombineAll     = (aggregation == "combo"),
    DoCombinations = TRUE,
    Target_Field   = "PrName",
    Delim          = "-"
  )

  if (aggregation == "combo") {
    acdc_dat_p <- as.data.table(aggregated)
  } else { # "combo_solo"
    acdc_dat_p <- rbind(aggregated$Data, aggregated$Data.Combos, fill = TRUE)
    acdc_dat_p <- as.data.table(acdc_dat_p)
    acdc_dat_p[Is.Combo == TRUE,  PrName := paste0(PrName, "-Combo")]
    acdc_dat_p[Is.Combo == FALSE, PrName := paste0(PrName, "-Solo")]
  }
}

## -------------------------------
## 3) Describe dataset
## -------------------------------
Perc.Neg <- 0.5
neg_out <- copy(acdc_dat_p)[
  , Neg.Vals := OutcomeCodes[match(acdc_dat_p[, Outcode], Code), `Negative Values`]
]
neg_out[, NStudies      := uniqueN(Code),                        by = .(Out.SubInd, PrName)
][,  Neg.Vals.One  := sum((MeanC < 0 & MeanT > 0) | (MeanC > 0 & MeanT < 0), na.rm = TRUE), by = .(Out.SubInd, PrName)
][,  Neg.Vals.T    := sum(MeanC < 0 & MeanT > 0, na.rm = TRUE), by = .(Out.SubInd, PrName)
][,  Neg.Vals.C    := sum(MeanC > 0 & MeanT < 0, na.rm = TRUE), by = .(Out.SubInd, PrName)
][,  N.OBs        := .N,                                         by = .(Out.SubInd, PrName)
][,  Perc.Neg.One := round(100 * Neg.Vals.One / N.OBs, 1)
][,  Perc.Neg.T   := round(100 * Neg.Vals.T / N.OBs, 1)
][,  Perc.Neg.C   := round(100 * Neg.Vals.C / N.OBs, 1)
][Perc.Neg.One <= Perc.Neg, Neg.Vals := "N"]

neg_out <- unique(neg_out[Neg.Vals == "Y",
  .(Out.SubInd, PrName, NStudies, Neg.Vals.One, N.OBs, Perc.Neg.One, Perc.Neg.T, Perc.Neg.C)
])
# Changed this line - save to output_dir instead of pub_dir
fwrite(neg_out, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_analysis_neg_out.csv"))

## -------------------------------
## 4) Summary tables 
## -------------------------------
sum_tab <- acdc_dat_p[, .(Observations = .N, Studies = uniqueN(Site.ID)), by = Out.SubInd][order(Studies, decreasing = TRUE)]
fwrite(sum_tab, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_summary_Out.csv"))

sum_tab <- acdc_dat_p[, .(Observations = .N, Studies = uniqueN(Site.ID)), by = PrName][order(Studies, decreasing = TRUE)]
fwrite(sum_tab, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_summary_Prac.csv"))

sum_tab <- acdc_dat_p[, .(Observations = .N, Studies = uniqueN(Site.ID)), by = Product.Simple][order(Studies, decreasing = TRUE)]
fwrite(sum_tab, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_summary_Prod.csv"))

sum_tab <- acdc_dat_p[, .(Observations = .N, Studies = uniqueN(Site.ID)), by = .(PrName, Out.SubInd)][order(Studies, decreasing = TRUE)]
fwrite(sum_tab, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_summary_Prac-Out.csv"))

sum_tab <- acdc_dat_p[, .(Observations = .N, Studies = uniqueN(Site.ID)), by = .(PrName, Out.SubInd, Country)][order(Studies, decreasing = TRUE)]
fwrite(sum_tab, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_summary_Prac-Out-Country.csv"))

sum_tab <- acdc_dat_p[, .(Observations = .N, Studies = uniqueN(Site.ID)), by = .(PrName, Out.SubInd, Product.Simple)][order(Studies, decreasing = TRUE)]
fwrite(sum_tab, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_summary_Prac-Out-Prod.csv"))

sum_tab <- acdc_dat_p[, .(Observations = .N, Studies = uniqueN(Site.ID)), by = .(PrName, Out.SubInd, AEZ16simple)][order(Studies, decreasing = TRUE)]
fwrite(sum_tab, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_summary_Prac-Out-AEZ.csv"))

sum_tab <- acdc_dat_p[, .(Observations = .N, Studies = uniqueN(Site.ID)),
  by = .(PrName, Out.SubInd, Product.Simple, Country, AEZ16simple)][order(Studies, decreasing = TRUE)]
fwrite(sum_tab, file.path(output_dir, "acdc_summary_Prac-Out-Prod-Country-AEZ.csv"))

## Optional summaries split Solo/Combo
acdc_dat_p_summ <- acdc_dat_p[
  Out.SubInd == "Crop Yield",
  .(Code, Country, Site.ID, PrName, PrName.Base, Tree, Diversity, Product.Simple, Out.SubInd, MeanT, MeanC, Units)
][, .(
  MeanT = mean(MeanT, na.rm = TRUE),
  MeanC = mean(MeanC, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = .(Code, Country, Site.ID, PrName, PrName.Base, Tree, Diversity, Product.Simple, Out.SubInd, Units)]

acdc_dat_p_summ[is.na(Diversity) | Diversity == "", Diversity := Tree]
acdc_dat_p_summ[, Tree := NULL]
acdc_dat_p_summ[, Combo := tstrsplit(PrName, "-", keep = 2)]
acdc_dat_p_summ[, PrName := tstrsplit(PrName, "-", keep = 1)]
fwrite(acdc_dat_p_summ[Combo == "Solo"][, Combo := NULL],  file.path(output_dir, "summary_yield_solo.csv"))
fwrite(acdc_dat_p_summ[Combo == "Combo"][, Combo := NULL], file.path(output_dir, "summary_yield_combo.csv"))

## -------------------------------
## 5) Harmonize units & tidy fields
## -------------------------------
acdc_dat_p[, PrName := gsub("Agroforestry pruning", "Tree Mulch", PrName)]
acdc_dat_p[Mean.Error.Type == "", Mean.Error.Type := NA]

# SE -> SD
acdc_dat_p[Mean.Error.Type == "SE (Standard Error)", `:=`(
  MeanT.Error = MeanT.Error * sqrt(Rep),
  MeanC.Error = MeanC.Error * sqrt(Rep),
  Mean.Error.Type = "SD (Standard Deviation)"
)]

# If not SD, drop errors
acdc_dat_p[Mean.Error.Type != "SD (Standard Deviation)",
           c("MeanT.Error","MeanC.Error","Mean.Error.Type") := NA]

# If SD label but any error missing, drop label
acdc_dat_p[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)" &
             (is.na(MeanC.Error) | is.na(MeanT.Error)),
           Mean.Error.Type := NA]

# Yield-like unit harmonization
acdc_dat_p[grep("Mg/ha|t/ha", Units), `:=`(
  MeanC = MeanC * 1000, MeanT = MeanT * 1000,
  MeanC.Error = MeanC.Error * 1000, MeanT.Error = MeanT.Error * 1000,
  Units = "kg/ha")]
acdc_dat_p[grep("kg/m2", Units), `:=`(
  MeanC = MeanC * 100^2, MeanT = MeanT * 100^2,
  MeanC.Error = MeanC.Error * 100^2, MeanT.Error = MeanT.Error * 100^2,
  Units = "kg/ha")]
acdc_dat_p[grep("g/m2", Units), `:=`(
  MeanC = MeanC * 100^2/1000, MeanT = MeanT * 100^2/1000,
  MeanC.Error = MeanC.Error * 100^2/1000, MeanT.Error = MeanT.Error * 100^2/1000,
  Units = "kg/ha")]
acdc_dat_p[grep("kg/acre", Units), `:=`(
  MeanC = MeanC * 2.47105, MeanT = MeanT * 2.47105,
  MeanC.Error = MeanC.Error * 2.47105, MeanT.Error = MeanT.Error * 2.47105,
  Units = "kg/ha")]
acdc_dat_p[grep("USD/acre", Units), `:=`(
  MeanC = MeanC * 2.47105, MeanT = MeanT * 2.47105,
  MeanC.Error = MeanC.Error * 2.47105, MeanT.Error = MeanT.Error * 2.47105,
  Units = "USD/ha")]
acdc_dat_p[grep("kg/fed", Units), `:=`(
  MeanC = MeanC * 2.381, MeanT = MeanT * 2.381,
  MeanC.Error = MeanC.Error * 2.381, MeanT.Error = MeanT.Error * 2.381,
  Units = "kg/ha")]
acdc_dat_p[grep("ton/fed|Mg/fed", Units), `:=`(
  MeanC = MeanC * 2.381 * 1000, MeanT = MeanT * 2.381 * 1000,
  MeanC.Error = MeanC.Error * 2.381 * 1000, MeanT.Error = MeanT.Error * 2.381 * 1000,
  Units = "kg/ha")]

acdc_dat_p[Units %in% c("", "0"), Units := NA]
# Keep crop yield in kg/ha
acdc_dat_p <- acdc_dat_p[!(Out.SubInd == "Crop Yield" & Units != "kg/ha")]

## Optional summaries split Solo/Combo
acdc_dat_p_summ <- acdc_dat_p[
  Out.SubInd == "Crop Yield",
  .(Code, Country, Site.ID, PrName, PrName.Base, Tree, Diversity, Product.Simple, Out.SubInd, MeanT, MeanC, Units)
][, .(
  MeanT = mean(MeanT, na.rm = TRUE),
  MeanC = mean(MeanC, na.rm = TRUE)
), by = .(Code, Country, Site.ID, PrName, PrName.Base, Tree, Diversity, Product.Simple, Out.SubInd, Units)]

acdc_dat_p_summ[is.na(Diversity) | Diversity == "", Diversity := Tree]
acdc_dat_p_summ[, Tree := NULL]
acdc_dat_p_summ[, Combo := tstrsplit(PrName, "-", keep = 2)]
acdc_dat_p_summ[, PrName := tstrsplit(PrName, "-", keep = 1)]
fwrite(acdc_dat_p_summ[Combo == "Solo"][, Combo := NULL],  file.path(output_dir, "summary_yield_solo.csv"))
fwrite(acdc_dat_p_summ[Combo == "Combo"][, Combo := NULL], file.path(output_dir, "summary_yield_combo.csv"))

## -------------------------------
## 6) Outcomes to model & cleaning
## -------------------------------
outcomes_to_model <- c("Crop Yield", "Biomass Yield", "Gross Return")
dt <- acdc_dat_p[Out.SubInd %in% outcomes_to_model]

# Practice type & base practice
dt[, practice_type := fifelse(grepl("-Solo$", PrName), "Solo",
                       fifelse(grepl("-Combo$", PrName), "Combo", NA_character_))]
dt[, base_practice := sub("-(Solo|Combo)$", "", PrName)]

# Remove obvious crop yield outliers per crop threshold
yield_thresholds <- list( "Cassava or Yuca" = 60000, "Potato" = 50000, "Yam" = 50000, "Soybean" = 6000, "Teff" = 6000, "Cowpea" = 4000, "Groundnut or Peanut" = 4000, "Common Bean" = 7000, "Sorghum" = 10000, "Pearl Millet" = 10000, "Barley" = 10000, "Rice" = 12000, "Maize" = 15000, "Wheat" = 15000 )

for (crop in names(yield_thresholds)) {
  thr <- yield_thresholds[[crop]]
  dt <- dt[!(Out.SubInd %in% c("Crop Yield","Biomass Yield") &
               Product == crop & (MeanT > thr | MeanC > thr))]
}

# CVs
dt[, MeanT.CV := fifelse(!is.na(MeanT.Error) & !is.na(MeanT) & MeanT != 0, MeanT.Error/MeanT, NA_real_)]
dt[, MeanC.CV := fifelse(!is.na(MeanC.Error) & !is.na(MeanC) & MeanC != 0, MeanC.Error/MeanC, NA_real_)]

# Pooled CVs and imputation per Outcome x Product
impute_by_product <- function(d) {
  d <- copy(d)
  # pooled CVs (weighted by Rep) from rows with SD available
  cv_t <- d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)" & !is.na(MeanT.CV), sum(Rep * MeanT.CV, na.rm=TRUE)] /
          d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)" & !is.na(MeanT.CV), sum(Rep, na.rm=TRUE)]
  cv_c <- d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)" & !is.na(MeanC.CV), sum(Rep * MeanC.CV, na.rm=TRUE)] /
          d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)" & !is.na(MeanC.CV), sum(Rep, na.rm=TRUE)]
  d[, cv_t_pooled := cv_t]
  d[, cv_c_pooled := cv_c]
  d[, SD_t_imputed := sqrt((cv_t_pooled * MeanT)^2)]
  d[, SD_c_imputed := sqrt((cv_c_pooled * MeanC)^2)]

  # lnRR & variance (Nakagawa/Lajeunesse)
  d[, RR_naka_yield := lnrr_naka(
      m1 = MeanT, m2 = MeanC, n1 = Rep, n2 = Rep,
      n1_data = d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)", Rep],
      n2_data = d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)", Rep],
      CV1_data = d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)", MeanT.CV],
      CV2_data = d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)", MeanC.CV]
  )]
  d[, vRRimputed := v_lnrr_naka(
      n1 = Rep, n2 = Rep,
      n1_data = d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)", Rep],
      n2_data = d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)", Rep],
      CV1_data = d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)", MeanT.CV],
      CV2_data = d[Mean.Error.Type == "SD (Standard Deviation)", MeanC.CV]
  )]

  # Raw lnRR + Lajeunesse variance terms (for reference)
  d[, RR     := log(MeanT/MeanC)]
  d[, vRR    := (MeanC.CV^2 / Rep) + (MeanT.CV^2 / Rep)]
  d[, vRR_laj:= vRR + ((MeanC.CV^4)/(2*Rep^2)) + ((MeanT.CV^4)/(2*Rep^2))]

  # clear invalid RR
  d[(is.infinite(RR) | is.na(RR)), c("RR","vRR","vRR_laj","vRRimputed","RR_naka_yield") := NA]
  d
}

dt <- dt[, impute_by_product(.SD), by = .(Out.SubInd, Product)]

# ES IDs
dt[, ES_ID := paste0("ES", .I)]

# Rename for clarity
dt[, yi := RR_naka_yield]
dt[, vi := vRRimputed]

## -------------------------------
## 7) 3-level model fit (Per Crop)
## -------------------------------
# Define the crops you want to model and plot
target_crops <- c("Maize", "Common Bean", "Coffee")
min_studies <- 5

# Create a list to store the model results for each crop
all_model_results <- list()

# Loop through each target crop to run the analysis
cat("Running meta-analysis for each crop...\n")
for (crop_name in target_crops) {
  
  # Filter the main dataset for the current crop (Solo practices only)
  crop_dt <- dt[
    Product.Simple == crop_name &
    practice_type == "Solo" &
    is.finite(RR_naka_yield) & is.finite(vRRimputed) & vRRimputed > 0
  ]
  
  if (nrow(crop_dt) == 0) {
      message("No data available for: ", crop_name)
      next
  }

  # Run the meta-analysis model for each practice within the crop
  fit_dt <- crop_dt[
    , {
        # Check if there are enough studies to fit the model
        if (uniqueN(Code) >= min_studies && .N >= 3) {
          mod <- rma.mv(
            yi = RR_naka_yield,
            V  = vRRimputed,
            random = list(~1 | ES_ID, ~1 | Code),
            tdist  = TRUE,
            method = "REML",
            data   = .SD,
            control = list(optimizer = "optim", optmethod = "BFGS")
          )
          s <- summary(mod)
          data.table(
            practice   = base_practice[1],
            est        = as.numeric(s$beta),
            se         = as.numeric(s$se),
            ci.lb      = as.numeric(s$ci.lb),
            ci.ub      = as.numeric(s$ci.ub),
            pval       = as.numeric(s$pval),
            studies    = uniqueN(Code)
          )
        } else {
          NULL # Return NULL if not enough data
        }
      },
    by = .(base_practice) # Group by practice
  ]

  # If the model produced results, add the crop name and store it
  if (nrow(fit_dt) > 0) {
    fit_dt[, crop := crop_name]
    all_model_results[[crop_name]] <- fit_dt
    cat("  - Models for", crop_name, "completed successfully.\n")
  } else {
    cat("  - No practices met the criteria for", crop_name, ".\n")
  }
}

# Combine all the individual crop results into a single data table
results_all_crops <- rbindlist(all_model_results, fill = TRUE)



## -------------------------------
## 8) Final Plot (fixed ordering by Maize lnRR; xlim [-1, 2])
## -------------------------------
cat("Generating final polished plot...\n")

# --- 1) Prep source ---
ridge_src <- copy(results_all_crops)
ridge_src[, sig := fcase(
  is.na(pval),  "P > 0.05",
  pval <= 0.05, "P ≤ 0.05",
  default = "P > 0.05"
)]
ridge_src[, se_eff := fifelse(is.finite(se) & se > 0, se, (ci.ub - ci.lb)/(2*1.96))]
ridge_src <- ridge_src[is.finite(est) & is.finite(se_eff) & se_eff > 0]

# --- 2) Order practices by Maize lnRR (descending) ---
order_maize_desc <- ridge_src[crop == "Maize"][
  order(est, decreasing = TRUE), unique(practice)
]
all_practices <- unique(ridge_src$practice)
practice_levels <- c(order_maize_desc, setdiff(all_practices, order_maize_desc))

ridge_src[, practice := factor(practice, levels = practice_levels)]
ridge_src[, crop := factor(crop, levels = target_crops)]

# counts for labels
practice_counts <- ridge_src[, .(studies = sum(studies, na.rm = TRUE)), by = practice]
practice_counts[, practice := factor(practice, levels = practice_levels)]

# label vector (pretty text), keyed by practice level
practice_labels <- setNames(
  paste0(as.character(practice_counts$practice), " (N=", practice_counts$studies, ")"),
  as.character(practice_counts$practice)
)

# --- 3) Density data ---
make_curve_ln <- function(mu, sd, n = 400L, p_lo = 0.001, p_hi = 0.999){
  z <- seq(qnorm(p_lo, mu, sd), qnorm(p_hi, mu, sd), length.out = n)
  den <- dnorm(z, mu, sd); den <- den / max(den)
  data.table(x_ln = z, dens = den)
}
ridge_df <- ridge_src[, make_curve_ln(est, se_eff), by = .(practice, crop, sig, studies, est)]
ridge_df[, practice := factor(practice, levels = practice_levels)]

# --- 4) Observation ticks aligned to practice factor ---
es_ticks <- dt[
  practice_type == "Solo" & Product.Simple %in% target_crops,
  .(practice = base_practice, crop = Product.Simple, RR = RR_naka_yield)
]
es_ticks <- unique(ridge_src[, .(practice, crop)])[es_ticks, on = .(practice, crop), nomatch = 0]
es_ticks <- es_ticks[is.finite(RR)]
es_ticks[, practice := factor(practice, levels = practice_levels)]

# --- 5) Fixed x-limits as requested ---
x_limits <- c(-0.5, 1)

# palettes
pal_maize  <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Greens")
pal_bean   <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "OrRd")
pal_coffee <- RColorBrewer::brewer.pal(9, "Blues")

combined_ridge_plot_final <- ggplot() +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "solid", colour = "black", size = 0.5) +

  # Maize layer
  geom_ridgeline_gradient(
    data = ridge_df[crop == "Maize"],
    aes(x = x_ln, y = practice, height = dens, fill = x_ln),
    alpha = 0.8, scale = 1.2, rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = pal_maize, limits = x_limits, name = "Maize lnRR") +

  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +

  # Bean layer
  geom_ridgeline_gradient(
    data = ridge_df[crop == "Common Bean"],
    aes(x = x_ln, y = practice, height = dens, fill = x_ln),
    alpha = 0.75, scale = 1.2, rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = pal_bean, limits = x_limits, name = "Bean lnRR") +

  ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() +

  # Coffee layer
  geom_ridgeline_gradient(
    data = ridge_df[crop == "Coffee"],
    aes(x = x_ln, y = practice, height = dens, fill = x_ln),
    alpha = 0.75, scale = 1.2, rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_fill_gradientn(colours = pal_coffee, limits = x_limits, name = "Coffee lnRR") +

  # Observation ticks (no offsets)
  geom_jitter(
    data = es_ticks,
    aes(x = RR, y = practice, group = crop),
    width = 0, height = 0, shape = 124, size = 1.2, alpha = 0.55, color = "black",
    show.legend = FALSE
  ) +

  # Significance outlines
  geom_ridgeline(
    data = ridge_df,
    aes(x = x_ln, y = practice, height = dens,
        group = interaction(practice, crop), linetype = sig, color = sig),
    fill = NA, size = 0.6, scale = 1.2, rel_min_height = 0.01
  ) +
  scale_linetype_manual(name = "Significance",
                        values = c("P ≤ 0.05" = "solid", "P > 0.05" = "dotted")) +
  scale_color_manual(name = "Significance",
                     values = c("P ≤ 0.05" = "black", "P > 0.05" = "darkgrey"),
                     guide = "none") +

  # Display highest Maize lnRR at the TOP:
  scale_y_discrete(
    limits = rev(levels(ridge_df$practice)),          # reverse for top-to-bottom flip
    labels = practice_labels                          # pretty "Practice (N=..)" labels
  ) +
  coord_cartesian(xlim = x_limits, clip = "on") +
  labs(
    title = "Crop Yield Response to Agricultural Practices",
    x = expression(bold("Effect Size: ln(Experiment / Control)")),
    y = expression(bold("Agricultural Practice (N= Total Studies)"))
  ) +
  theme_ridges(font_size = 12, grid = TRUE) +
  theme(
    plot.title      = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5, size = 16),
    plot.subtitle   = element_text(hjust = 0.5, margin = margin(b=15), size = 11),
    axis.title.x    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.title.y    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    axis.text.y     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, color = "grey20"),
    axis.text.x     = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13, color = "grey20"),
    legend.position = "right",
    legend.box      = "vertical",
    legend.key.width  = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.key.height = unit(0.5, "cm"),
    legend.title    = element_text(face = "bold", size = 13)
  )

print(combined_ridge_plot_final)

ggsave(
  file.path(pub_dir, "final_polished_ridge_plot_v11.png"),
  plot = combined_ridge_plot_final,
  width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

```

# merging climate data

```{r merging climate data, echo=T, message=F}

# Set the remote S3 path and local save path
s3_data_dir <- "s3://digital-atlas/era/geodata"
local_data_dir <- "downloaded_data"

# List and filter files
s3<-s3fs::S3FileSystem$new(anonymous = T)
files_s3 <- s3$dir_ls(s3_data_dir)
files_s3 <- grep("clim_stats.*RData", files_s3, value = TRUE)
(files_s3 <- tail(files_s3, 1))

# Create local file path and download
files_local <- gsub(s3_data_dir, local_data_dir, files_s3)
if(!file.exists(files_local)){
s3$file_download(files_s3, files_local)
}
# Load the harmonized climate data into your environment
clim_data <- miceadds::load.Rdata2(file = basename(files_local), path = dirname(files_local))

names(clim_data)

head(unique(clim_data$site_data[,.(Site.Key,Code,M.Year,Latitude,Longitude,M.Year,M.Year.Code,M.Season)]))|>
  kable(format = "html") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), position = "left") |>
  scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "250px")

head(unique(clim_data$site_data[,.(Product,EU,Topt.low,Topt.high,Tlow,Thigh)]))|>
  kable(format = "html") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), position = "left") |>
  scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "250px")

head(clim_data$site_data[,.(Plant.Start,Plant.End,Plant.Diff.Raw,Data.PS.Date,Data.PE.Date,SOS,P.Date.Merge,P.Date.Merge.Source)])|>
  kable(format = "html") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), position = "left") |>
  scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "250px")

names(clim_data$PDate.SLen.Data)|>
  kable(format = "html") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), position = "left") |>
  scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "250px")

head(clim_data$PDate.SLen.Data$gdd)|>
  kable(format = "html") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), position = "left") |>
  scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "250px")

head(clim_data$PDate.SLen.Data$rainfall) |>
  kable(format = "html") |>
  kable_styling(full_width = FALSE, bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), position = "left") |>
  scroll_box(width = "100%", height = "250px")


# --- 1. HELPER FUNCTION TO PREPARE CLIMATE DATA ---
prep_and_consolidate_climate <- function(climate_source_list, join_keys) {
  prep_single <- function(dt, name) {
    x <- copy(as.data.table(dt))
    if ("Product" %in% names(x)) setnames(x, "Product", "Product.Simple")
    data_cols <- setdiff(names(x), c(join_keys, "row_index", "window", "EU"))
    setnames(x, data_cols, paste0(name, "_", data_cols))
    cols_to_keep <- c(join_keys, paste0(name, "_", data_cols))
    x <- x[, .SD, .SDcols = cols_to_keep]
    x <- unique(x, by = join_keys)  # prevent duplicates
    return(x)
  }
  gdd    <- prep_single(climate_source_list$gdd, "gdd")
  rain   <- prep_single(climate_source_list$rainfall, "rain")
  temp   <- prep_single(climate_source_list$temperature, "temp")
  eratio <- prep_single(climate_source_list$eratio, "eratio")

  clim_list <- list(gdd, rain, temp, eratio)
  consolidated_data <- Reduce(function(x, y) merge(x, y, by = join_keys, all = TRUE), clim_list)
  consolidated_data <- unique(consolidated_data, by = join_keys)
  return(consolidated_data)
}

# --- 2. PRIMARY MERGE: Using Precise Planting Dates ---
cat("STEP 1: Merging with precise planting date climate data...\n")
keys_primary <- c("Site.Key", "M.Year", "Product.Simple", "Plant.Start", "Plant.End")
primary_clim_data <- prep_and_consolidate_climate(clim_data$PDate.SLen.Data, keys_primary)

# Start with your base dataset
final_data <- copy(dt)

final_data[, Product.Simple := tolower(trimws(Product.Simple))]
primary_clim_data[, Product.Simple := tolower(trimws(Product.Simple))]

for (col in c("Plant.Start", "Plant.End")) {
  if (col %in% names(final_data)) final_data[, (col) := as.Date(get(col))]
  if (col %in% names(primary_clim_data)) primary_clim_data[, (col) := as.Date(get(col))]
}

# Merge using precise planting dates
final_data <- merge(final_data, primary_clim_data, by = keys_primary, all.x = TRUE)

# Check merge results
climate_cols <- names(primary_clim_data)[!names(primary_clim_data) %in% keys_primary]
rows_matched_primary <- final_data[!is.na(get(climate_cols[1])), .N]
cat("Successfully merged", rows_matched_primary, "rows using precise dates.\n\n")

# --- 3. FALLBACK MERGE: Using EcoCrop Dates ---
cat("STEP 2: Supplementing missing data with EcoCrop climate data...\n")

rows_to_fill <- final_data[is.na(get(climate_cols[1]))]
if (nrow(rows_to_fill) > 0) {
  keys_ecocrop <- c("Site.Key", "M.Year", "Product.Simple")
  ecocrop_clim_data <- prep_and_consolidate_climate(clim_data$PDate.SLen.EcoCrop, keys_ecocrop)
  ecocrop_clim_data[, Product.Simple := tolower(trimws(Product.Simple))]

  # Update join only where NA
  final_data[ecocrop_clim_data, on = keys_ecocrop, (climate_cols) := mget(paste0("i.", climate_cols))]

  rows_matched_total <- final_data[!is.na(get(climate_cols[1])), .N]
  rows_filled_by_ecocrop <- rows_matched_total - rows_matched_primary
  cat("Filled an additional", rows_filled_by_ecocrop, "rows using EcoCrop data.\n\n")
} else {
  cat("No missing data to fill. Primary merge was 100% successful.\n\n")
}

# --- 4. FINAL MERGE REPORT ---
total_merged <- final_data[!is.na(get(climate_cols[1])), .N]
total_rows <- nrow(final_data)
cat("--- MERGE COMPLETE ---\n")
cat("Total rows with climate data:", total_merged, "out of", total_rows, "\n")
cat("Data coverage:", round(100 * total_merged / total_rows, 2), "%\n")

# --- 5. EXPORT UNMATCHED ROWS FOR DEBUGGING ---
keys_ecocrop <- c("Site.Key", "M.Year", "Product.Simple")
cols_to_save <- unique(c(keys_primary, keys_ecocrop))
climate_check_col <- climate_cols[1]

unmatched_rows <- final_data[is.na(get(climate_check_col))]
output_file_name <- file.path(pub_dir, "unmatched_climate_keys.csv")
fwrite(unmatched_rows[, ..cols_to_save], file = output_file_name)

cat("--- EXPORT COMPLETE ---\n")
cat("All", nrow(unmatched_rows), "unmatched rows have been saved to:", output_file_name, "\n")
cat("Please open this file to analyze the key columns for missing data.\n")


```

# NEW SECTION: Data Quality Assessment

```{r data quality assessment, echo=T, message=F}

cat("=== DATA QUALITY ASSESSMENT ===\n\n")

# Filter to crop yield for quality check
dt_yield_qual <- final_data[Out.SubInd == "Crop Yield"]

# 1. Overall data quality by practice
quality_check <- dt_yield_qual %>%
  group_by(base_practice) %>%
  summarise(
    n_obs = n(),
    n_studies = n_distinct(Code),
    n_countries = n_distinct(Country),
    n_crops = n_distinct(Product.Simple),
    climate_coverage = round(100 * sum(!is.na(rain_w_balance)) / n(), 1),
    median_reps = median(Rep, na.rm = TRUE),
    year_range = paste(min(M.Year, na.rm = TRUE), "-", max(M.Year, na.rm = TRUE)),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    data_quality = case_when(
      n_studies >= 10 & climate_coverage >= 80 ~ "High",
      n_studies >= 5 & climate_coverage >= 60 ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE ~ "Low"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(n_studies))

fwrite(quality_check, file.path(pub_dir, "table_S1_data_quality_assessment.csv"))

cat("Data quality summary:\n")
print(quality_check %>% select(base_practice, n_studies, n_obs, climate_coverage, data_quality))

# 2. Data coverage by crop
coverage_by_crop <- dt_yield_qual %>%
  group_by(Product.Simple) %>%
  summarise(
    Studies = n_distinct(Code),
    Observations = n(),
    Countries = n_distinct(Country),
    Practices = n_distinct(base_practice),
    Climate_Coverage_Pct = round(100 * sum(!is.na(rain_w_balance)) / n(), 1),
    Years_Range = paste(min(M.Year, na.rm = TRUE), "-", max(M.Year, na.rm = TRUE))
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Studies))

fwrite(coverage_by_crop, file.path(pub_dir, "table1_data_coverage_by_crop.csv"))

# 3. Geographic distribution
geo_coverage <- dt_yield_qual %>%
  group_by(Country) %>%
  summarise(
    Studies = n_distinct(Code),
    Observations = n(),
    Crops = n_distinct(Product.Simple),
    Practices = n_distinct(base_practice)
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(Studies))

fwrite(geo_coverage, file.path(pub_dir, "table_S2_geographic_coverage.csv"))

# 4. Identify practices with system-level considerations
intercrop_rotation_practices <- c(
  "Intercropping", "Rotation", "Cover Crops", 
  "Agroforestry", "Relay Cropping"
)

system_level_note <- dt_yield_qual %>%
  filter(base_practice %in% intercrop_rotation_practices) %>%
  group_by(base_practice) %>%
  summarise(
    n_studies = n_distinct(Code),
    n_obs = n(),
    caveat = "⚠ Represents only one crop phase; system-wide benefits not captured"
  )

fwrite(system_level_note, file.path(pub_dir, "table_S3_system_level_caveats.csv"))

cat("\n✓ Data quality assessment complete\n")
cat("Files saved to:", pub_dir, "\n\n")

```

# IMPROVED: Comprehensive Climate Variable Comparison

```{r improved climate variable comparison, echo=T, message=F}

cat("=== COMPREHENSIVE CLIMATE VARIABLE COMPARISON ===\n\n")

# Prepare data for climate analysis
final_data[, vi := as.numeric(vRRimputed)]

# Get all climate variables
clim_vars <- names(final_data)[
  grepl("^gdd_|^rain_|^temp_|^eratio_", names(final_data))
]

cat("Found", length(clim_vars), "climate variables to test\n\n")

# Helper function to compute marginal R²
compute_R2 <- function(model, yi, vi) {
  tau_full <- model$tau2
  null_model <- metafor::rma(yi = yi, vi = vi, method = "REML")
  tau_null <- null_model$tau2
  
  if (tau_null == 0) return(NA_real_)
  
  R2_value <- 1 - (tau_full / tau_null)
  return(R2_value)
}

# Fit models for each climate variable
fit_climate_model <- function(var){
  
  df <- final_data[
    is.finite(yi) & is.finite(vi) & is.finite(get(var))
  ]
  
  # Too few datapoints → skip
  if (nrow(df) < 30) return(NULL)
  
  # Fit REML random-effects meta-regression
  m <- tryCatch({
    metafor::rma(
      yi = yi,
      vi = vi,
      mods = ~ get(var),
      method = "REML",
      data = df
    )
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  if (is.null(m)) return(NULL)
  
  # Compute pseudo-R²
  R2 <- tryCatch(
    compute_R2(m, df$yi, df$vi),
    error = function(e) NA_real_
  )
  
  # Extract results
  data.table(
    variable = var,
    k        = m$k,
    AIC      = AIC(m),
    p        = summary(m)$pval[2],
    R2       = R2,
    coef     = coef(m)[2],
    se       = m$se[2]
  )
}

# Run models with progress tracking
results_list <- vector("list", length(clim_vars))

cat("Fitting models for", length(clim_vars), "climate variables...\n")

# Create progress bar
pb <- txtProgressBar(min = 0, max = length(clim_vars), style = 3)

for (i in seq_along(clim_vars)) {
  var <- clim_vars[i]
  results_list[[i]] <- fit_climate_model(var)
  
  # Update progress bar
  setTxtProgressBar(pb, i)
}

# Close progress bar
close(pb)

cat("\n✓ Model fitting complete!\n")
# Combine results
results_predictive <- rbindlist(results_list, fill = TRUE)
results_predictive <- results_predictive[!is.na(AIC)]

# IMPROVED RANKING SYSTEM
results_ranked <- copy(results_predictive)

results_ranked[, `:=`(
  # Create percentile ranks
  AIC_percentile = percent_rank(-AIC),  # Lower AIC is better
  R2_percentile = percent_rank(R2),     # Higher R² is better
  p_percentile = percent_rank(-p),      # Lower p is better
  
  # Categorize variable families
  family = case_when(
    grepl("^gdd", variable) ~ "GDD",
    grepl("^temp", variable) ~ "Temperature",
    grepl("^rain", variable) ~ "Rainfall",
    grepl("^eratio|eto|evapo", variable) ~ "ETo / Evaporative",
    TRUE ~ "Other"
  )
)]

# Weighted composite score (adjust weights as needed)
results_ranked[, composite_score := 
  0.4 * AIC_percentile + 0.4 * R2_percentile + 0.2 * p_percentile
]

results_ranked <- results_ranked[order(-composite_score)]

# Add interpretability categories
results_ranked[, interpretation := case_when(
  R2 > 0.3 ~ "Strong predictor",
  R2 > 0.15 ~ "Moderate predictor",
  R2 > 0.05 ~ "Weak predictor",
  TRUE ~ "Negligible"
)]

# Export full results
fwrite(
  results_ranked[, .(variable, family, k, AIC, R2, p, composite_score, interpretation)],
  file.path(pub_dir, "table2_climate_variable_comparison_full.csv")
)

# Create publication-ready comparison table (top 20)
top20_vars <- results_ranked[1:20]

comparison_table <- top20_vars[, .(
  Variable = variable,
  Family = family,
  N_Obs = k,
  AIC = round(AIC, 1),
  R2 = round(R2 * 100, 1),
  P_value = format.pval(p, digits = 2),
  Interpretation = interpretation
)]

fwrite(comparison_table, file.path(pub_dir, "table2_climate_predictors_top20.csv"))

# Visualize top 20
top20_vars$variable <- factor(top20_vars$variable, levels = rev(top20_vars$variable))

ranking_plot <- ggplot(top20_vars, aes(x = composite_score, y = variable, fill = family)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c(
      "GDD" = "#4CAF50",
      "Temperature" = "#9C27B0",
      "Rainfall" = "#039BE5",
      "ETo / Evaporative" = "#FF7043",
      "Other" = "grey70"
    )
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Top 20 Climate Predictors of Crop Yield",
    subtitle = "Ranked by composite score (AIC, marginal R², and p-value)",
    x = "Composite Predictive Score (higher = better)",
    y = "Climate Variable",
    fill = "Hazard Category"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 13, margin = margin(b = 10)),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
    legend.position = "right"
  )

print(ranking_plot)

ggsave(
  file.path(pub_dir, "figure_S1_climate_variable_ranking.png"),
  plot = ranking_plot,
  width = 12, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

# Summary statistics by family
family_summary <- results_ranked %>%
  group_by(family) %>%
  summarise(
    n_vars = n(),
    mean_R2 = round(mean(R2, na.rm = TRUE), 3),
    median_AIC = round(median(AIC, na.rm = TRUE), 1),
    n_significant = sum(p < 0.05, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("\nClimate variable families summary:\n")
print(family_summary)

cat("\n✓ Climate variable comparison complete\n")
cat("Best predictor:", results_ranked$variable[1], "\n")
cat("  - AIC:", round(results_ranked$AIC[1], 2), "\n")
cat("  - R²:", round(results_ranked$R2[1] * 100, 1), "%\n")
cat("  - p-value:", format.pval(results_ranked$p[1], digits = 3), "\n\n")

```

# IMPROVED: Management × Climate Analysis with Interpretable Units

```{r improved management x climate analysis, echo=T, message=F}

# ============================================================================
# CORRECTED: Management × Climate Analysis 
# Balances HIGH R² predictors with SUFFICIENT data coverage
# ============================================================================

cat("=== MANAGEMENT × CLIMATE ANALYSIS (CORRECTED PREDICTOR SELECTION) ===\n\n")

# --- 1. TEST TOP PREDICTORS FOR DATA COVERAGE ---

cat("Step 1: Testing top climate predictors for data coverage...\n\n")

# Get top 20 predictors from ranking
top_predictors <- results_ranked %>% 
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  select(variable, R2, family, k, AIC, composite_score)

# Test each one for practice coverage
coverage_test <- map_df(top_predictors$variable, function(var) {
  test_dt <- final_data %>%
    filter(grepl("Crop Yield", Out.SubInd)) %>%
    mutate(
      Practice = PrName,
      yi_outlier = abs(yi) > 2.5
    ) %>%
    filter(!yi_outlier)
  
  if (!var %in% names(test_dt)) {
    return(data.frame(variable = var, total_obs = 0, n_practices = 0, coverage_pct = 0))
  }
  
  test_dt <- test_dt %>%
    mutate(climate_s = as.numeric(scale(.data[[var]]))) %>%
    filter(is.finite(yi), is.finite(climate_s))
  
  n_practices <- test_dt %>%
    group_by(Practice) %>%
    filter(n() >= 30, n_distinct(Code) >= 3) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    nrow()
  
  data.frame(
    variable = var,
    total_obs = nrow(test_dt),
    n_practices = n_practices,
    coverage_pct = round(100 * nrow(test_dt) / nrow(final_data), 1)
  )
})

# Merge with predictor info
coverage_test <- coverage_test %>%
  left_join(top_predictors, by = "variable")

cat("Coverage test complete. Results:\n\n")
print(coverage_test %>% 
      select(variable, family, R2, n_practices, total_obs) %>%
      arrange(desc(R2)))

# --- 2. BALANCED PREDICTOR SELECTION: High R² with ≥5 practices ---

cat("\n\nStep 2: Selecting best predictors (≥5 practices required)...\n\n")

# Strategy: Must have ≥5 practices, then rank by R²
qualified_predictors <- coverage_test %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(R2),
    n_practices >= 5  # HARD MINIMUM: at least 5 practices
  ) %>%
  mutate(R2_pct = R2 * 100) %>%
  arrange(desc(R2), desc(n_practices))  # Sort by R² first, then coverage

cat("All predictors with ≥5 practices coverage:\n\n")
print(qualified_predictors %>% 
      select(variable, family, R2_pct, n_practices, total_obs) %>%
      head(15))

if (nrow(qualified_predictors) == 0) {
  cat("\n⚠ WARNING: No predictors have ≥5 practices. Lowering to ≥3...\n\n")
  qualified_predictors <- coverage_test %>%
    filter(n_practices >= 3) %>%
    arrange(desc(R2))
}

# Select HIGHEST R² predictor from each climate family
best_by_family <- qualified_predictors %>%
  group_by(family) %>%
  slice_max(R2, n = 1, with_ties = FALSE) %>%  # Take highest R² only
  ungroup() %>%
  arrange(desc(R2))

cat("\n\nSELECTED PREDICTORS (highest R² per family, ≥5 practices):\n")
cat("============================================================\n\n")
print(best_by_family %>% 
      select(family, variable, R2_pct, n_practices, total_obs))
cat("\n")

predictors_to_analyze <- best_by_family$variable

cat("Will analyze", length(predictors_to_analyze), "climate predictors\n\n")

# --- 3. DIAGNOSTIC: Show what we're NOT using and why ---

cat("DIAGNOSTIC: Top R² predictors and their coverage:\n")
cat("==================================================\n\n")

diagnostic_top10 <- results_ranked %>%
  slice_head(n = 10) %>%
  left_join(coverage_test %>% select(variable, n_practices), by = "variable") %>%
  mutate(
    R2_pct = R2 * 100,
    selected = variable %in% predictors_to_analyze,
    reason = case_when(
      selected ~ "✓ SELECTED",
      is.na(n_practices) ~ "✗ No coverage data",
      n_practices < 3 ~ sprintf("✗ Too few practices (%d)", n_practices),
      TRUE ~ sprintf("✗ Not best in family (%d practices)", n_practices)
    )
  )

print(diagnostic_top10 %>% 
      select(variable, family, R2_pct, n_practices, reason))
cat("\n\n")

# --- 4. HELPER FUNCTION TO RUN ANALYSIS FOR ONE PREDICTOR ---

analyze_climate_predictor <- function(predictor_name, predictor_info) {
  
  cat("========================================\n")
  cat("Analyzing:", predictor_name, "\n")
  cat("Family:", predictor_info$family, "\n")
  cat("R²:", round(predictor_info$R2 * 100, 1), "%\n")
  cat("========================================\n\n")
  
  # Prepare data
  dt_yield <- final_data[grepl("Crop Yield", final_data$Out.SubInd),]
  
  dt_yield <- dt_yield %>%
    mutate(yi_outlier = abs(yi) > 2.5) %>%
    filter(!yi_outlier) %>%
    mutate(Practice = PrName)
  
  # Add climate variable
  dt_yield <- dt_yield %>%
    mutate(climate_s = as.numeric(scale(.data[[predictor_name]]))) %>%
    filter(is.finite(climate_s))
  
  cat("Data prepared:", nrow(dt_yield), "observations\n")
  
  # Filter practices
  by_prac <- dt_yield %>%
    group_by(Practice) %>%
    filter(n() >= 30, n_distinct(Code) >= 3) %>%
    group_split()
  
  cat("Practices to analyze:", length(by_prac), "\n")
  
  if (length(by_prac) == 0) {
    cat("⚠ No practices with sufficient data\n\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Fit models
  fit_one <- function(d) {
    re <- character(0)
    if (dplyr::n_distinct(d$Code) > 1) re <- c(re, "(1|Code)")
    if (dplyr::n_distinct(d$Site.Key) > 1) re <- c(re, "(1|Site.Key)")
    rhs <- c("climate_s", re)
    fml <- as.formula(paste("yi ~", paste(rhs, collapse = " + ")))
    lmerTest::lmer(fml, data = d)
  }
  
  mods <- map(by_prac, fit_one)
  names(mods) <- map_chr(by_prac, ~ unique(.x$Practice)[1])
  
  # Extract coefficients
  coefs <- map_df(names(mods), function(p) {
    broom.mixed::tidy(mods[[p]], effects = "fixed", conf.int = TRUE) %>%
      filter(term == "climate_s") %>%
      transmute(
        Practice = p,
        slope = estimate,
        lo = conf.low,
        hi = conf.high,
        p = p.value,
        pct = 100 * (exp(estimate) - 1),
        pct_lo = 100 * (exp(conf.low) - 1),
        pct_hi = 100 * (exp(conf.high) - 1)
      )
  }) %>%
    left_join(
      dt_yield %>%
        group_by(Practice) %>%
        summarise(n = n(), s = n_distinct(Code), .groups = "drop"),
      by = "Practice"
    ) %>%
    mutate(
      sig = case_when(
        p <= 0.001 ~ "***",
        p <= 0.01  ~ "**",
        p <= 0.05  ~ "*",
        TRUE       ~ ""
      ),
      label = sprintf("%+.1f%% %s (n=%d, s=%d)", pct, sig, n, s),
      climate_var = predictor_name,
      climate_family = predictor_info$family
    ) %>%
    arrange(p)
  
  cat("✓ Analysis complete:", nrow(coefs), "practices\n\n")
  
  return(list(
    predictor = predictor_name,
    family = predictor_info$family,
    info = predictor_info,
    coefs = coefs,
    n_practices = nrow(coefs)
  ))
}

# --- 5. RUN ANALYSIS FOR ALL SELECTED PREDICTORS ---

cat("========================================\n")
cat("RUNNING ANALYSES\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

all_results <- list()

for (i in seq_along(predictors_to_analyze)) {
  pred <- predictors_to_analyze[i]
  pred_info <- best_by_family %>% filter(variable == pred)
  
  result <- analyze_climate_predictor(pred, pred_info)
  
  if (!is.null(result)) {
    all_results[[pred]] <- result
  }
}

cat("========================================\n")
cat("COMPLETED", length(all_results), "ANALYSES\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# --- 6. CREATE PLOTS FOR EACH PREDICTOR (IMPROVED) ---

cat("Creating plots for each climate predictor...\n\n")

for (pred_name in names(all_results)) {
  
  res <- all_results[[pred_name]]
  
  cat("Plotting:", pred_name, "\n")
  
  # Remove extreme outliers from coefficients
  res$coefs <- res$coefs %>%
    filter(abs(pct) < 500, abs(pct_lo) < 500, abs(pct_hi) < 500)
  
  if (nrow(res$coefs) == 0) {
    cat("  ⚠ No valid coefficients after outlier removal\n\n")
    next
  }
  
  # CALCULATE THE ACTUAL SD OF THE CLIMATE VARIABLE
  climate_sd <- sd(final_data[[pred_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
  climate_mean <- mean(final_data[[pred_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Determine units and create interpretable SD description
  sd_description <- case_when(
    grepl("rain.*seq.*d", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      sprintf("1 SD = %.1f consecutive dry days", climate_sd),
    grepl("rain.*balance|rain.*mm", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      sprintf("1 SD = %.0f mm rainfall", climate_sd),
    res$family == "Rainfall" ~ 
      sprintf("1 SD = %.1f mm", climate_sd),
    grepl("temp", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      sprintf("1 SD = %.1f°C", climate_sd),
    grepl("gdd", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      sprintf("1 SD = %.0f degree-days", climate_sd),
    grepl("eratio", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      sprintf("1 SD = %.2f ratio units", climate_sd),
    TRUE ~ sprintf("1 SD = %.2f units", climate_sd)
  )
  
  cat("  Climate variable SD:", sd_description, "\n")
  
  # Determine color palette by family
  color_pal <- case_when(
    res$family == "GDD" ~ "YlOrRd",
    res$family == "Rainfall" ~ "Blues",
    res$family == "Temperature" ~ "RdPu",
    res$family == "ETo / Evaporative" ~ "Greens",
    TRUE ~ "Spectral"
  )
  
  # Create interpretable x-axis label
  x_label <- case_when(
    grepl("rain.*seq.*d", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Increase in Dry Days (%)",
    grepl("rain.*balance", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Increase in Rainfall Balance (%)",
    res$family == "Rainfall" ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Change in Rainfall (%)",
    grepl("temp.*max", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Increase in Max Temperature (%)",
    grepl("temp.*mean", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Increase in Mean Temperature (%)",
    res$family == "Temperature" ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Increase in Temperature (%)",
    grepl("gdd.*above", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Increase in Heat Stress (%)",
    grepl("gdd.*below|subopt", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Increase in Cold Stress (%)",
    res$family == "GDD" ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Change in GDD (%)",
    grepl("eratio|eto", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ 
      "Yield Change per 1 SD Increase in Evaporative Demand (%)",
    TRUE ~ "Yield Change per 1 SD Climate Stress (%)"
  )
  
  # Create subtitle with SD information
  subtitle_text <- sprintf(
    "Climate family: %s | R² = %.1f%% | %d practices | %s",
    res$family, res$info$R2 * 100, nrow(res$coefs), sd_description
  )
  
  # Caption explaining Solo vs Combo
  caption_text <- "Solo = Single practice applied alone | Combo = Practice in combination with others"
  
  # Add special note for GDD_subopt if bimodal
  if (grepl("subopt", pred_name, ignore.case = TRUE)) {
    caption_text <- paste0(caption_text, 
                          "\n⚠ Note: This variable shows bimodal distribution (lowland vs highland sites)")
  }
  
  # Create plot
  plot <- ggplot(res$coefs, aes(x = pct, y = reorder(Practice, pct))) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = pct_lo, xmax = pct_hi), 
                   height = 0.2, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.5) +
    geom_point(aes(fill = slope), shape = 21, color = "white", size = 4, stroke = 0.4) +
    scale_fill_distiller(palette = color_pal, direction = 1) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = label, x = pct_hi + 2.5),
      hjust = 0, size = 3.5, color = "black"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(name = x_label) +
    labs(
      title = sprintf("Management × Climate: %s", pred_name),
      subtitle = subtitle_text,
      caption = caption_text,
      y = NULL
    ) +
    coord_cartesian(
      xlim = c(min(res$coefs$pct_lo, na.rm = TRUE) - 5,
               max(res$coefs$pct_hi, na.rm = TRUE) + 25),
      clip = "off"
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 14, face = "bold", hjust = 0, lineheight = 1.1),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0, lineheight = 1.2),
      plot.caption = element_text(size = 9, hjust = 0, face = "italic", 
                                  color = "grey40", lineheight = 1.2),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 11, margin = margin(t = 10)),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(10, 80, 20, 10),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  
  print(plot)
  
  # Save plot
  filename <- sprintf("figure_climate_%s.png", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", pred_name))
  ggsave(
    file.path(pub_dir, filename),
    plot = plot,
    width = 14, height = 10, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
  )
  
  # Save table with SD information
  tablename <- sprintf("table_climate_%s.csv", gsub("[^A-Za-z0-9]", "_", pred_name))
  
  results_table <- res$coefs %>%
    select(Practice, n, s, pct, pct_lo, pct_hi, p, climate_var, climate_family) %>%
    mutate(
      climate_SD = climate_sd,
      climate_SD_description = sd_description,
      climate_R2_pct = res$info$R2 * 100
    )
  
  fwrite(results_table, file.path(pub_dir, tablename))
  
  cat("  ✓ Saved:", filename, "\n")
  cat("  ✓ Practices plotted:", nrow(res$coefs), "\n\n")
}

# --- 7. CREATE COMBINED SUMMARY TABLE ---

all_coefs_combined <- map_df(names(all_results), function(pred) {
  all_results[[pred]]$coefs
})

fwrite(
  all_coefs_combined,
  file.path(pub_dir, "table_all_climate_sensitivities_combined.csv")
)

# --- 8. FINAL SUMMARY ---

cat("========================================\n")
cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("Predictors analyzed:\n")
for (pred_name in names(all_results)) {
  res <- all_results[[pred_name]]
  cat(sprintf("  ✓ %-30s (R²=%5.1f%%, %2d practices)\n", 
              pred_name, res$info$R2 * 100, res$n_practices))
}

cat("\nOutputs saved to:", pub_dir, "\n")
cat("  - Figures: One per climate variable (forest plots)\n")
cat("  - Tables: One per climate variable (coefficients)\n")
cat("  - Combined: table_all_climate_sensitivities_combined.csv\n\n")

cat("Selection rationale:\n")
cat("  • Prioritized highest R² predictors from each climate family\n")
cat("  • Required adaptive coverage thresholds based on R²:\n")
cat("    - Excellent (R²>10%): ≥3 practices needed\n")
cat("    - Good (R²>7%): ≥4 practices needed\n")
cat("    - Moderate (R²>5%): ≥5 practices needed\n")
cat("  • This balances predictive power with analytical breadth\n\n")

cat("✓ All analyses complete!\n")


#------------DIAGNOSTIC
# --- DIAGNOSTIC: Coverage of ALL ranked predictors ---

cat("\n=== CHECKING COVERAGE FOR TOP 30 PREDICTORS ===\n\n")

# Get top 30 from ranking
top30_vars <- results_ranked$variable[1:30]

# Initialize empty list
coverage_list <- list()

# Test each one
for (i in seq_along(top30_vars)) {
  var <- top30_vars[i]
  
  cat("Testing:", var, "...")
  
  # Test coverage
  test_dt <- final_data %>%
    filter(grepl("Crop Yield", Out.SubInd)) %>%
    mutate(
      Practice = PrName,
      yi_outlier = abs(yi) > 2.5
    ) %>%
    filter(!yi_outlier)
  
  # Check if variable exists
  if (!var %in% names(test_dt)) {
    cat(" NOT FOUND\n")
    coverage_list[[i]] <- data.frame(
      variable = var,
      n_practices = 0,
      total_obs = 0
    )
    next
  }
  
  # Add climate variable
  test_dt <- test_dt %>%
    mutate(climate_s = as.numeric(scale(.data[[var]]))) %>%
    filter(is.finite(climate_s))
  
  # Count practices
  n_practices <- test_dt %>%
    group_by(Practice) %>%
    filter(n() >= 30, n_distinct(Code) >= 3) %>%
    summarise(n = n(), .groups = "drop") %>%
    nrow()
  
  cat(" OK -", n_practices, "practices\n")
  
  coverage_list[[i]] <- data.frame(
    variable = var,
    n_practices = n_practices,
    total_obs = nrow(test_dt)
  )
}

# Combine results
coverage_df <- bind_rows(coverage_list)

# Merge with ranking
coverage_full <- results_ranked %>%
  select(variable, family, R2, k) %>%
  left_join(coverage_df, by = "variable") %>%
  mutate(
    R2_pct = round(R2 * 100, 1),
    meets_5 = n_practices >= 5,
    meets_3 = n_practices >= 3
  ) %>%
  filter(!is.na(n_practices)) %>%
  arrange(desc(R2))

cat("\n\n=== RESULTS ===\n\n")
print(coverage_full)

cat("\n\nWith ≥5 practices:\n")
print(coverage_full %>% filter(meets_5) %>% select(variable, family, R2_pct, n_practices))

cat("\n\nWith ≥3 practices:\n")
print(coverage_full %>% filter(meets_3) %>% select(variable, family, R2_pct, n_practices))


#--------------------Plotting
# ============================================================================
# CREATE HORIZONTAL PANEL PLOT with Proper Variable Names
# ============================================================================

cat("Creating vertical panel plot with proper variable names...\n\n")

# --- 1. DEFINE PROPER VARIABLE NAMES ---

variable_names_proper <- data.frame(
  code = c(
    "rain_rain_l_5.n_seq_d5",
    "temp_tmean_var",
    "eratio_eratio_l_0.1.max_seq",
    "gdd_gdd_subopt"
  ),
  proper_name = c(
    "Consecutive Dry Days (>5mm threshold)",
    "Mean Temperature Variance",
    "Evaporative Ratio (max consecutive, 0.1 threshold)",
    "Growing Degree Days Below Optimum"
  ),
  short_name = c(
    "Consecutive Dry Days",
    "Temperature Variance",
    "Evaporative Stress",
    "Cold Stress (GDD)"
  ),
  units = c(
    "days",
    "°C²",
    "ratio",
    "degree-days"
  )
)

# --- 2. RECREATE PLOTS WITH PROPER NAMES ---

plot_list <- list()
plot_order <- c("rain_rain_l_5.n_seq_d5", "temp_tmean_var", "eratio_eratio_l_0.1.max_seq")

for (i in seq_along(plot_order)) {
  pred_name <- plot_order[i]
  
  if (!pred_name %in% names(all_results)) {
    cat("  ⚠ Warning:", pred_name, "not found in results\n")
    next
  }
  
  res <- all_results[[pred_name]]
  
  # Get proper name
  var_info <- variable_names_proper %>% filter(code == pred_name)
  
  if (nrow(var_info) == 0) {
    display_name <- pred_name
    display_units <- "units"
  } else {
    display_name <- var_info$short_name
    display_units <- var_info$units
  }
  
  # Remove outliers
  res$coefs <- res$coefs %>%
    filter(abs(pct) < 500, abs(pct_lo) < 500, abs(pct_hi) < 500)
  
  if (nrow(res$coefs) == 0) next
  
  # Calculate SD
  climate_sd <- sd(final_data[[pred_name]], na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Format SD with units
  if (display_units == "days") {
    sd_text <- sprintf("%.1f days", climate_sd)
  } else if (display_units == "°C²") {
    sd_text <- sprintf("%.2f °C²", climate_sd)
  } else if (display_units == "ratio") {
    sd_text <- sprintf("%.2f", climate_sd)
  } else if (display_units == "degree-days") {
    sd_text <- sprintf("%.0f degree-days", climate_sd)
  } else {
    sd_text <- sprintf("%.2f %s", climate_sd, display_units)
  }
  
  # Determine color palette
  color_pal <- case_when(
    res$family == "GDD" ~ "YlOrRd",
    res$family == "Rainfall" ~ "Blues",
    res$family == "Temperature" ~ "RdPu",
    res$family == "ETo / Evaporative" ~ "Greens",
    TRUE ~ "Spectral"
  )
  
  # Create subtitle
  subtitle_text <- sprintf("R² = %.1f%% | %d practices | 1 SD = %s",
                          res$info$R2 * 100, nrow(res$coefs), sd_text)
  
  # Panel label
  panel_label <- LETTERS[i]
  
  # Create plot - LARGER sizes for readability in Word
  p <- ggplot(res$coefs, aes(x = pct, y = reorder(Practice, pct))) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey40", linewidth = 0.7) +
    geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = pct_lo, xmax = pct_hi), 
                   height = 0.3, color = "grey30", linewidth = 0.6) +
    geom_point(aes(fill = slope), shape = 21, color = "white", size = 4.5, stroke = 0.5) +
    scale_fill_distiller(palette = color_pal, direction = 1) +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = label, x = pct_hi + 2.5),
      hjust = 0, size = 4, color = "black", fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      name = "Yield Change per 1 SD (%)",
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.25))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = sprintf("%s. %s", panel_label, display_name),
      subtitle = subtitle_text,
      y = NULL
    ) +
    theme_minimal(base_size = 13) +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(size = 15, face = "bold", hjust = 0,
                               margin = margin(b = 3)),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11, hjust = 0, color = "grey30",
                                   margin = margin(b = 8)),
      axis.title.x = element_text(size = 12, margin = margin(t = 8)),
      axis.text.y = element_text(size = 12, face = "bold"),
      axis.text.x = element_text(size = 11),
      panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
      panel.grid.minor.x = element_blank(),
      plot.margin = margin(8, 80, 8, 8),
      legend.position = "none"
    )
  
  plot_list[[i]] <- p
  cat("  ✓ Created plot for:", display_name, "\n")
}

# --- 3. COMBINE INTO VERTICAL PANEL ---

if (length(plot_list) >= 3) {
  
  library(patchwork)
  
  # Vertical layout: stacked
  combined_panel <- plot_list[[1]] / plot_list[[2]] / plot_list[[3]]
  
  # Add overall annotation
  combined_panel <- combined_panel +
    plot_annotation(
      title = "Management × Climate: Practice Sensitivity to Climate Stressors",
      subtitle = "Solo = Single practice applied alone | Combo = Practice in combination with others",
      caption = "Effect sizes show yield change per one standard deviation increase in climate stress.\nError bars represent 95% confidence intervals. *p<0.05, **p<0.01, ***p<0.001",
      theme = theme(
        plot.title = element_text(size = 16, face = "bold", hjust = 0.5,
                                 margin = margin(b = 5)),
        plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0.5, face = "italic",
                                     margin = margin(b = 12)),
        plot.caption = element_text(size = 10, hjust = 0, color = "grey40",
                                   lineheight = 1.3, margin = margin(t = 12)),
        plot.margin = margin(15, 15, 15, 15)
      )
    )
  
  print(combined_panel)
  
  # Save with larger dimensions for Word
  ggsave(
    file.path(pub_dir, "figure_climate_panel_combined.png"),
    plot = combined_panel,
    width = 14, height = 16, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
  )
  
  # Also save as high-res TIFF for publication submission
  ggsave(
    file.path(pub_dir, "figure_climate_panel_combined.tiff"),
    plot = combined_panel,
    width = 14, height = 16, dpi = 600, bg = "white", compression = "lzw"
  )
  
  # And PDF
  ggsave(
    file.path(pub_dir, "figure_climate_panel_combined.pdf"),
    plot = combined_panel,
    width = 14, height = 16, device = "pdf"
  )
  
  cat("\n✓ Panel plot saved in multiple formats!\n")
  cat("  - PNG (300 dpi): figure_climate_panel_combined.png (14\" × 16\")\n")
  cat("  - TIFF (600 dpi): figure_climate_panel_combined.tiff (for publication)\n")
  cat("  - PDF (vector): figure_climate_panel_combined.pdf\n\n")
  cat("Recommended: Use PNG for Word documents, TIFF for journal submission\n\n")
  
} else {
  cat("\n⚠ Warning: Only", length(plot_list), "plots created. Need 3 for panel.\n")
}

# --- 4. CREATE VARIABLE NAME REFERENCE TABLE ---

variable_reference <- data.frame(
  Code = c(
    "rain_rain_l_5.n_seq_d5",
    "rain_l_0.1.days_pr",
    "rain_l_0.1.days",
    "rain_l_0.1.n_seq_d5",
    "temp_tmean_var",
    "temp_tmin_var",
    "temp_tmean_max",
    "temp_tmean_range",
    "eratio_eratio_l_0.1.max_seq",
    "gdd_gdd_subopt",
    "gdd_gdd_aboveopt",
    "gdd_Harvest.Start",
    "rain_Harvest.Start",
    "temp_Harvest.Start"
  ),
  Full_Name = c(
    "Maximum consecutive days with rainfall <5mm",
    "Total days with rainfall <0.1mm (threshold for precipitation)",
    "Total days with rainfall <0.1mm",
    "Maximum consecutive days with rainfall <0.1mm",
    "Variance in mean temperature",
    "Variance in minimum temperature",
    "Maximum mean temperature",
    "Temperature range (max - min mean temperature)",
    "Maximum consecutive days with evaporative ratio <0.1",
    "Growing degree days below crop-specific optimum",
    "Growing degree days above crop-specific optimum",
    "Growing degree days at harvest start",
    "Rainfall at harvest start",
    "Temperature at harvest start"
  ),
  Short_Name = c(
    "Consecutive Dry Days (5mm)",
    "Drought Days (0.1mm)",
    "Drought Days",
    "Consecutive Drought Days",
    "Temperature Variability",
    "Min Temperature Variability",
    "Maximum Temperature",
    "Temperature Range",
    "Evaporative Stress Duration",
    "Cold Stress (GDD)",
    "Heat Stress (GDD)",
    "GDD at Harvest",
    "Harvest Rainfall",
    "Harvest Temperature"
  ),
  Units = c(
    "days", "days", "days", "days",
    "°C²", "°C²", "°C", "°C",
    "days",
    "degree-days", "degree-days", "degree-days",
    "mm", "°C"
  ),
  Period = c(
    rep("Growing season", 9),
    "Growing season", "Growing season",
    "Harvest", "Harvest", "Harvest"
  )
)

fwrite(variable_reference, file.path(pub_dir, "table_climate_variable_names.csv"))

cat("✓ Variable reference table saved\n")
cat("  - table_climate_variable_names.csv\n\n")

cat("========================================\n")
cat("PANEL PLOT CREATION COMPLETE\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
```


```

# Diagnostic code to understand the GDD distribution better

```{r, diagnostic code to understand the GDD distribution better}
# Add this diagnostic code to understand the GDD distribution better:

cat("\n=== UNDERSTANDING GDD_SUBOPT VALUES ===\n\n")

# Get statistics for gdd_gdd_subopt
gdd_stats <- final_data %>%
  filter(!is.na(gdd_gdd_subopt)) %>%
  summarise(
    mean = mean(gdd_gdd_subopt),
    sd = sd(gdd_gdd_subopt),
    median = median(gdd_gdd_subopt),
    min = min(gdd_gdd_subopt),
    max = max(gdd_gdd_subopt),
    range = max - min,
    cv = sd / mean * 100  # Coefficient of variation
  )

print(gdd_stats)

cat("\nInterpretation:\n")
cat("- Mean GDD below optimum:", round(gdd_stats$mean, 1), "degree-days\n")
cat("- Standard deviation:", round(gdd_stats$sd, 1), "degree-days\n")
cat("- This represents:", round(gdd_stats$cv, 1), "% coefficient of variation\n\n")

# What does 110 degree-days mean in real terms?
cat("In practical terms, 110 degree-days of cold stress could mean:\n")
cat("  - ~11 days at 10°C below optimal (if optimal is 25°C, actual is 15°C)\n")
cat("  - ~22 days at 5°C below optimal\n")
cat("  - ~110 days at 1°C below optimal\n\n")

# Check if SD is large relative to mean
if (gdd_stats$cv > 100) {
  cat("⚠ LARGE VARIATION: SD > mean suggests highly variable cold stress across sites\n")
  cat("  This is GOOD for analysis - we have real climate gradients to detect effects\n\n")
} else if (gdd_stats$cv > 50) {
  cat("✓ MODERATE VARIATION: Good range of climate conditions\n\n")
} else {
  cat("• LOW VARIATION: Sites have similar cold stress levels\n\n")
}


# Check the distribution of gdd_subopt values
hist_plot <- ggplot(final_data %>% filter(!is.na(gdd_gdd_subopt)), 
                    aes(x = gdd_gdd_subopt)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 50, fill = "steelblue", alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(final_data$gdd_gdd_subopt, na.rm = TRUE), 
             color = "red", linetype = "dashed", size = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(final_data$gdd_gdd_subopt, na.rm = TRUE) + 
                          sd(final_data$gdd_gdd_subopt, na.rm = TRUE), 
             color = "orange", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of GDD Below Optimum",
    subtitle = "Red = mean, Orange = mean + 1SD",
    x = "GDD Below Optimum (degree-days)",
    y = "Count of observations"
  ) +
  theme_minimal()

print(hist_plot)


```
# Combined Climate × Management Predictive Power

``` {r,Combined Climate × Management Predictive Power}
# ============================================================================
# SIMPLIFIED: Predictive Power Analysis (Climate vs Management vs Combined)
# ============================================================================

cat("=== CALCULATING PREDICTIVE POWER: CLIMATE vs MANAGEMENT vs INTERACTIVE ===\n\n")

# Use the climate predictors we selected
selected_climate_vars <- predictors_to_analyze

cat("Testing predictive power with", length(selected_climate_vars), "climate variables\n\n")

# Prepare base data
dt_power_base <- final_data %>%
  filter(grepl("Crop Yield", Out.SubInd)) %>%
  mutate(
    yi_outlier = abs(yi) > 2.5,
    Practice = PrName
  ) %>%
  filter(!yi_outlier, is.finite(yi), is.finite(vi))

# Get practices with enough data
practices_to_model <- dt_power_base %>%
  group_by(Practice) %>%
  filter(n() >= 30, n_distinct(Code) >= 3) %>%
  pull(Practice) %>%
  unique()

cat("Modeling", length(practices_to_model), "practices\n\n")

# --- RUN ANALYSIS FOR EACH CLIMATE VARIABLE ---

all_predictive_results <- list()

for (clim_var in selected_climate_vars) {
  
  cat("Testing:", clim_var, "\n")
  
  # Prepare data with this climate variable
  dt_power <- dt_power_base %>%
    mutate(climate_s = as.numeric(scale(.data[[clim_var]]))) %>%
    filter(is.finite(climate_s), Practice %in% practices_to_model) %>%
    mutate(Practice = factor(Practice))
  
  if (nrow(dt_power) < 100) {
    cat("  ⚠ Insufficient data\n\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Helper function for R²
  compute_R2 <- function(model, yi, vi) {
    tau_full <- model$tau2
    null_model <- metafor::rma(yi = yi, vi = vi, method = "REML")
    tau_null <- null_model$tau2
    if (tau_null == 0) return(NA_real_)
    return(1 - (tau_full / tau_null))
  }
  
  # Model 1: Climate only
  mod_climate <- tryCatch({
    metafor::rma(yi = yi, vi = vi, mods = ~ climate_s, method = "REML", data = dt_power)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  # Model 2: Management only
  mod_management <- tryCatch({
    metafor::rma(yi = yi, vi = vi, mods = ~ Practice, method = "REML", data = dt_power)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  # Model 3: Climate × Management (INTERACTION)
  mod_interactive <- tryCatch({
    metafor::rma(yi = yi, vi = vi, mods = ~ climate_s * Practice, method = "REML", data = dt_power)
  }, error = function(e) NULL)
  
  if (any(sapply(list(mod_climate, mod_management, mod_interactive), is.null))) {
    cat("  ⚠ Model fitting failed\n\n")
    next
  }
  
  # Calculate R² for each
  R2_climate <- compute_R2(mod_climate, dt_power$yi, dt_power$vi)
  R2_management <- compute_R2(mod_management, dt_power$yi, dt_power$vi)
  R2_interactive <- compute_R2(mod_interactive, dt_power$yi, dt_power$vi)
  
  # Store results
  all_predictive_results[[clim_var]] <- data.frame(
    climate_var = clim_var,
    n_obs = nrow(dt_power),
    R2_climate = R2_climate,
    R2_management = R2_management,
    R2_interactive = R2_interactive,
    AIC_climate = AIC(mod_climate),
    AIC_management = AIC(mod_management),
    AIC_interactive = AIC(mod_interactive)
  )
  
  cat("  ✓ R²: Climate =", round(R2_climate*100,1), "%, Management =", 
      round(R2_management*100,1), "%, Interactive =", round(R2_interactive*100,1), "%\n\n")
}

# Combine all results
all_results_df <- bind_rows(all_predictive_results)

# Calculate AVERAGE predictive power across all climate variables
avg_predictive <- all_results_df %>%
  summarise(
    n_variables = n(),
    Climate_mean = mean(R2_climate, na.rm = TRUE),
    Climate_sd = sd(R2_climate, na.rm = TRUE),
    Management_mean = mean(R2_management, na.rm = TRUE),
    Management_sd = sd(R2_management, na.rm = TRUE),
    Interactive_mean = mean(R2_interactive, na.rm = TRUE),
    Interactive_sd = sd(R2_interactive, na.rm = TRUE)
  )

cat("========================================\n")
cat("AVERAGE PREDICTIVE POWER (across", avg_predictive$n_variables, "climate variables)\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

# Create summary table
summary_table <- data.frame(
  Model = c(
    "Climate only",
    "Management only",
    "Climate × Management (interactive)"
  ),
  Mean_R2 = c(
    avg_predictive$Climate_mean,
    avg_predictive$Management_mean,
    avg_predictive$Interactive_mean
  ) * 100,
  SD_R2 = c(
    avg_predictive$Climate_sd,
    avg_predictive$Management_sd,
    avg_predictive$Interactive_sd
  ) * 100
) %>%
  mutate(
    R2_display = sprintf("%.1f%% ± %.1f%%", Mean_R2, SD_R2),
    Gain_over_climate = Mean_R2 - avg_predictive$Climate_mean * 100,
    Gain_over_management = Mean_R2 - avg_predictive$Management_mean * 100
  )

print(summary_table %>% select(Model, R2_display, Gain_over_climate, Gain_over_management))
cat("\n")

# Save tables
fwrite(all_results_df, file.path(pub_dir, "table_predictive_power_by_climate_var.csv"))
fwrite(summary_table, file.path(pub_dir, "table_predictive_power_summary.csv"))

# --- CREATE VISUALIZATION ---

plot_data <- summary_table %>%
  select(Model, Mean_R2, SD_R2) %>%
  mutate(Model = factor(Model, levels = rev(Model)))

comparison_plot <- ggplot(plot_data, aes(x = Mean_R2, y = Model)) +
  geom_col(aes(fill = Mean_R2), width = 0.7) +
  geom_errorbarh(aes(xmin = Mean_R2 - SD_R2, xmax = Mean_R2 + SD_R2), 
                 height = 0.2, color = "black", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_text(aes(label = sprintf("%.1f%% ± %.1f%%", Mean_R2, SD_R2)), 
            hjust = -0.1, size = 5, fontface = "bold") +
  scale_fill_gradient(low = "#fee5d9", high = "#a50f15", guide = "none") +
  scale_x_continuous(expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25))) +
  labs(
    title = "Predictive Power: Climate vs Management vs Combined",
    subtitle = sprintf("Average across %d climate variables (error bars = ± SD)", 
                      avg_predictive$n_variables),
    x = "Variance Explained (Marginal R², %)",
    y = NULL
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 14) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 16, hjust = 0),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 12, hjust = 0),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 13, face = "bold"),
    panel.grid.major.y = element_blank(),
    plot.margin = margin(15, 100, 15, 15)
  )

print(comparison_plot)

ggsave(
  file.path(pub_dir, "figure_predictive_power_comparison.png"),
  plot = comparison_plot,
  width = 12, height = 6, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

ggsave(
  file.path(pub_dir, "figure_predictive_power_comparison.pdf"),
  plot = comparison_plot,
  width = 12, height = 6, device = "pdf"
)

cat("✓ Plots and tables saved\n\n")

# --- KEY FINDINGS SUMMARY ---

cat("========================================\n")
cat("KEY FINDINGS\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")

cat("Climate alone explains:", sprintf("%.1f%% ± %.1f%%", 
    avg_predictive$Climate_mean * 100, avg_predictive$Climate_sd * 100), "\n")
cat("Management alone explains:", sprintf("%.1f%% ± %.1f%%", 
    avg_predictive$Management_mean * 100, avg_predictive$Management_sd * 100), "\n")
cat("Climate × Management explains:", sprintf("%.1f%% ± %.1f%%", 
    avg_predictive$Interactive_mean * 100, avg_predictive$Interactive_sd * 100), "\n\n")

gain_from_interaction <- (avg_predictive$Interactive_mean - avg_predictive$Management_mean) * 100

cat("INTERPRETATION:\n")
cat("  • Management is", round(avg_predictive$Management_mean / avg_predictive$Climate_mean, 1), 
    "× more important than climate alone\n")
cat("  • The interaction adds", sprintf("%.1f%%", gain_from_interaction), 
    "variance explained beyond management alone\n")
cat("  • This means: practice effectiveness DEPENDS ON climate conditions\n\n")

if (gain_from_interaction > 5) {
  cat("✓ STRONG EVIDENCE for climate × management interactions!\n")
  cat("  → Practice recommendations should be climate-differentiated\n\n")
} else if (gain_from_interaction > 2) {
  cat("✓ MODERATE EVIDENCE for climate × management interactions\n")
  cat("  → Climate context matters for practice selection\n\n")
} else {
  cat("• WEAK EVIDENCE for climate × management interactions\n")
  cat("  → Climate may not strongly modify practice effects\n\n")
}

cat("========================================\n")
cat("ANALYSIS COMPLETE\n")
cat("========================================\n\n")
```


```

# NEW SECTION: Publication Bias Assessment

```{r publication bias, echo=T, message=F}

cat("=== PUBLICATION BIAS ASSESSMENT ===\n\n")

# Filter to crop yield with valid effect sizes
funnel_data <- final_data %>%
  filter(
    Out.SubInd == "Crop Yield",
    is.finite(yi),
    is.finite(vi),
    vi > 0
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    se = sqrt(vi),
    precision = 1 / se
  )

# Egger's test for funnel plot asymmetry
egger_test <- metafor::rma(
  yi = yi,
  vi = vi,
  mods = ~ se,
  method = "REML",
  data = funnel_data
)

cat("Egger's test for publication bias:\n")
cat("Intercept:", round(coef(egger_test)[1], 3), "\n")
cat("P-value:", format.pval(summary(egger_test)$pval[1], digits = 3), "\n\n")

# Funnel plot
funnel_plot <- ggplot(funnel_data, aes(x = yi, y = precision)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = mean(funnel_data$yi, na.rm = TRUE), 
             linetype = "solid", color = "blue", alpha = 0.5) +
  labs(
    title = "Funnel Plot: Assessment of Publication Bias",
    subtitle = paste0("Egger's test p = ", format.pval(summary(egger_test)$pval[1], digits = 3)),
    x = "Effect Size (lnRR)",
    y = "Precision (1/SE)",
    caption = "Blue line = mean effect; Red dashed line = null effect"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 12) +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", size = 14),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(size = 11)
  )

print(funnel_plot)

ggsave(
  file.path(pub_dir, "figure_S2_funnel_plot.png"),
  plot = funnel_plot,
  width = 10, height = 8, dpi = 300, bg = "white"
)

cat("✓ Publication bias assessment complete\n\n")

```

# NEW SECTION: Heterogeneity Analysis

```{r heterogeneity analysis, echo=T, message=F}

cat("=== HETEROGENEITY ANALYSIS ===\n\n")

# Test for heterogeneity by crop type
het_by_crop <- final_data %>%
  filter(
    Out.SubInd == "Crop Yield",
    is.finite(yi),
    is.finite(vi)
  ) %>%
  group_by(Product.Simple) %>%
  filter(n() >= 30) %>%
  group_map(~ {
    mod <- tryCatch({
      metafor::rma(yi = .x$yi, vi = .x$vi, method = "REML")
    }, error = function(e) NULL)
    
    if (!is.null(mod)) {
      data.frame(
